tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi there! I'm trying to get back into the swing of things with our community group. Could you help me find any memories or notes I've saved about our members' preferences and past interactions? I want to make sure I'm up to speed before our next gathering.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "community group members preferences past interactions gathering"}

messages.content: I searched for memories about community group members and interactions, but didn't find any matches. Let me check if there are any memories stored at all for you:

It looks like you don't have any memories saved yet in the system. That's no problem at all!

Here's what I can help with:

- **Save memories**: If you'd like to remember member preferences, past conversation topics, or any details about your community gatherings, just tell me and I can store those for you.
- **Search later**: Once memories are saved, I can help you search and retrieve them anytime.

Would you like to start adding some notes about your community group? For example, you could tell me about member names, their interests, or anything else you'd like to remember for your next gathering!

That sounds great! I'd love to start building up our community notes. I remember from our last gathering that Maria mentioned she really enjoys icebreaker activities that get people sharing personal stories, rather than just quick introductions. She said something about how she opened up more to the group after we did that "two truths and a wish" exercise. Could you save that for me?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Community group member preference: Maria enjoys icebreaker activities that get people sharing personal stories rather than quick introductions. She mentioned she opened up more to the group after doing the \"two truths and a wish\" exercise at the last gathering."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Community group member preference: Maria enjoys icebreaker activities that get people sharing personal stories rather than quick introductions. She mentioned she opened up more to the group after doing the \"two truths and a wish\" exercise at the last gathering."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Got it! I've saved Maria's preference for story-based icebreakers, specifically noting how much she enjoyed the "two truths and a wish" exercise. Now you'll have it handy for your next gathering!

Would you like to add any more notes about other members or past activities?
That would be wonderful, thank you! Now that we've added Maria's preference, could you show me all the memories I've stored so far? I'd like to review what's been collected before our next gathering.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Community group member preference: Maria enjoys icebreaker activities that get people sharing personal stories rather than quick introductions. She mentioned she opened up more to the group after doing the \"two truths and a wish\" exercise at the last gathering."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what you've stored so far:

1. **Maria's preference** – She enjoys icebreaker activities that get people sharing personal stories rather than quick introductions. She mentioned she opened up more to the group after doing the "two truths and a wish" exercise at the last gathering.

That's the only memory in the system right now. Would you like to add more notes about other members or anything else before your next gathering?
I can see we only have Maria's preference saved, which is still relevant and useful. Is there anything in the collection that seems outdated or should be removed to keep things current?

Looking at the current collection, Maria's preference doesn't seem outdated at all—it's directly relevant to planning your next gathering. Her preference for story-based icebreakers like "two truths and a wish" is still useful information that you can apply going forward.

Since there's only one memory saved, there's nothing to remove at this point. The system is pretty minimal right now!

If you're thinking of any other details that might be outdated (maybe from a much earlier gathering, or something that no longer applies), feel free to let me know and I can help remove those. Otherwise, would you like to add more notes about other members?

structured_turns.goal_text: Search for existing memories about community group preferences and past interactions
structured_turns.user_message: Hi there! I'm trying to get back into the swing of things with our community group. Could you help me find any memories or notes I've saved about our members' preferences and past interactions? I want to make sure I'm up to speed before our next gathering.
structured_turns.assistant_message:
It looks like you don't have any memories saved yet in the system. That's no problem at all!

Here's what I can help with:

- **Save memories**: If you'd like to remember member preferences, past conversation topics, or any details about your community gatherings, just tell me and I can store those for you.
- **Search later**: Once memories are saved, I can help you search and retrieve them anytime.

Would you like to start adding some notes about your community group? For example, you could tell me about member names, their interests, or anything else you'd like to remember for your next gathering!
